rationale: Same trigger-alignment skeleton as before (bind per-run names, peel `next_ensures` conjuncts, instantiate the `well_typed_events` per-index foralls with explicit triggers, flatten `er.view()` into its record shape), but the NetClient view flattening now matches the actual `view` body: `NetClientView { history: self.history@, end_point: self.end_point }`. The previous attempt used `post_netc.history()` (the unrelated closed external_body spec method) for the `history` field, which Verus could not equate with the Ghost-field projection `self.history@` and thus rejected; switching to `post_netc.history@` makes the assertion a direct unfolding of the closed `view` body.
